title: Bad Run-Through
summary: Benchmarking the extent of ICT development is an important tool for policy-makers. It allows comparisons between countries and indicates how well countries are doing compared to others in terms of adaptation, mastery and development. Comparison with better-performing countries helps identify policies for further improvement and progression, which forms the major subject of Panel III. Cross-country analyses without benchmarking the extent of development generally lack the depth of insight required for policy purposes.
body: A lesson in unpreparedness The latest from Belfer See more Benchmarking the extent of ICT development is an important tool for policy-makers. It allows comparisons between countries and indicates how well countries are doing compared to others in terms of adaptation, mastery and development. Comparison with better-performing countries helps identify policies for further improvement and progression, which forms the major subject of Panel III. Cross-country analyses without benchmarking the extent of development generally lack the depth of insight required for policy purposes. Bad Run-Through Print Energy Technology Innovation Policy Science, Technology, and Public Policy
